awesomeList.descriptionHTML: A curated list of helpful Heroku resources.
awesomeList.description: A curated list of helpful Heroku resources.

nodes.itemHTML: • article Deploying Django to Heroku With Docker — looks at how to deploy a Django app to Heroku with Docker via the Heroku Container Runtime
• article Split Frontend from Backend on Heroku with npm and NodeJS - hands-on guide to deploy a microservices web application in Heroku using npm and NodeJS.
• article Six Tips for Mastering your Procfile — improvements for how you run your Heroku processes.
• blog Heroku Blog — the official Heroku blog.
• book Heroku Cookbook — step-by-step recipes to solve the challenges of administering and scaling a real-world production web application on Heroku.
• official Preboot — explains how to use the "Preboot" feature to enable zero-downtime deployments, which can be tricky to get right.
• plugin heroku-buildpack-pgbouncerstars317 — a buildpack that allows for transaction pooling using stunnel and pgbouncer to avoid hitting connection limits.
• official Managing Multiple Environments for an App — a good primer on how to think about managing the different pieces of each environment.
• talk Buildpack Adventure — a cool slideshow about Heroku's buildpacks, and what the open-source community is hacking together with them.
• article Exploring Microservices Architecure on Heroku — explores why Heroku eliminates a lot of the tooling you need to worry about when working with microservices.
• article Configuring CloudFlare DNS for a Heroku app — a walkthrough of how to use CloudFlare as your DNS provider.
• book The Heroku Hacker's Guide — an ebook that covers a lot of the basics in maintaining and scaling a project on Heroku.
• official Heroku Postgres — the landing page explaining with it is.
• article Heroku and SOA — discusses why Heroku is perfectly suited to building a service-oriented architecture for your projects.
• question How do you ignore files when deploying to Heroku? — the answer to a common question about .gitignore like functionality.

• question How do you host multiple Heroku apps on a single domain? — a StackOverflow question with a few responses to a the common question of how to serve multiple Heroku apps from different paths instead of subdomains.
• official The big kickoff — the first ever Heroku blog article.
• article Heroku isn't for idiots — explains Heroku's advantages and why it's not just for side projects.
• article My Heroku values — a great series of takeaways from Brandur Leach when he left Heroku.
• tool HireFire — a SaaS tool that automatically scales your Heroku dynos up and down as load requires.
• tool dokku — a self-hosted, docker-based, Heroku-compliant platform.
• article Hosting multiple Heroku apps on a single domain — an article on how to share the same domain between multiple Heroku applications.

• article Automating our Heroku deployments from Jenkins — explains how you'd go about automating certain parts of deployment like enabling maintenance mode, copying your database from production to staging, running migrations against staging, etc.
• article Set up CloudFlare's free SSL on Heroku — walks you through the exact steps to setting up free SSL via Cloudflare.
• official Toolbelt Download — where to download the Heroku toolbelt.
• plugin heroku-accountsstars1.3k — makes it easy to work with multiple accounts at once from the command line.

• plugin heroku-pg-extrasstars1.2k — a toolbelt plugin that adds extra useful plugins for working with Postgres. Things like analyzing cache hit rates, outlier queries, unused indexes, table sizes, etc.
